reviews.review_text:
I love this game, but if you're considering purchasing it, it's important that you know what it is and what it is not. If you are looking for the kind of game with heavy player-animal interaction, this is probably not the game for you. If you want to do minimal management, this may not be the game for you. If you do not like design and construction (and I mean, a LOT of design and construction), this is not for you.

All that said, this is probably the best zoo simulator to ever exist. The amount of specificity, customization, realism, and detail is incredible, especially with architecture. Every animal has its own requirements and genetics. Guests and staff have a slew of different needs. You can map and track just about any metric possible. Years ago, I played Zoo Tycoon 2 extensively and was looking for a "new version," and this is a more than worthy successor. Some of the mechanics feel as if they were an answer to something I felt like Zoo Tycoon 2 lacked.

Not sure if you want something totally open-ended? You can still have a good time with it. I have exclusively played on career mode (and am trying a timed one). Currently, I have completed all base game career "levels" and am working my way through the DLC ones. Some of them involve taking over a pre-built zoo, some of them involve building a zoo from scratch, but you have to complete certain objectives to "win." I like having specific goals to work toward, and if that's the kind of thing you like, then you can still have fun. However, I think many players prefer sandbox, franchise, or challenge modes, which allow for even greater creativity.

Something to know is that the game does not give you much in the way of a tutorial. The tutorial is very barebones, and you have to figure out most of the complicated stuff yourself. Fortunately, people have made videos online that can help with this. Additionally, there are a lot of complicated tools and menus, and the construction process itself can be difficult and challenging to master. If you are the kind of person who will lose enjoyment in the game by seeing other players' intricate designs while you struggle to build a half-interesting enclosure, proceed with caution. But for me, I'm happy with my simpler designs and have slowly been learning more complicated things as I gain experience. Workshop blueprints others make can help with some of the complicated construction.

Of course, I have my minor gripes. The pathing is notoriously difficult, and terrain shaping can be hard. Animals can be finnicky, guests can be unreasonably whiny, and staff can be as smart as a rock. But these are small issues I can work around, and the fun I have designing and managing my zoos more than makes up for that. This game has pushed my creativity, and I notice and appreciate architecture, design, and zoos as a whole even more after playing this. And of course, I've learned quite a bit about different animals. Until Planet Zoo 2 comes out, I don't think you are going to find a more in-depth, customizable zoo simulator.

The ultimate paradise for animal lovers and creative architects!

Rating: 👍 Recommended (10/10)

If you have ever dreamed of building your own zoo, forget about everything else. Planet Zoo by Frontier Developments is not just a regular simulator – it is an absolute masterpiece that has no competition in the tycoon genre. I have spent hundreds of hours in this game, and I am still fascinated by how much it has to offer.

🐘 Animals That Breathe with Life
What will completely blow you away in the first few minutes is the incredible detail of the animals. Every lion, elephant, penguin, or tiny chameleon looks and behaves like a living creature. Their fur, skin texture, realistic movement animations, and social interactions are simply breathtaking. Watching lion cubs play or bears swim in the water is an incredibly therapeutic experience. The animal AI is top-notch – they react to the environment, visitor noise, weather changes, and require complex care.

🏗️ Endless and Unmatched Building Options
If you love building, Planet Zoo is literally a digital Lego set with unlimited possibilities. The construction system is so deep and sophisticated that it can be mind-blowing. You can control literally every single piece of wood, stone, wall, or roof.

Terrain Deformation: Create massive caves, waterfalls, underground tunnels for guests, or hills covered with lush vegetation.

Piece-by-Piece Building: Don't want pre-made shops? Build your own modern restaurant, an African village, or a futuristic research center.

Botanical Paradise: A vast variety of plants, trees, and flowers from all over the world allows you to create a habitat so authentic that you’ll forget you are in a game.

The game gives you absolute freedom. If you have enough patience, you can create visual masterpieces that look like they belong in the real world. And if you don't feel like building everything from scratch, the Steam Workshop is filled with thousands of amazing creations from the community.

💼 Deep Management and Gameplay
The game is not just about looks; it’s also about brains. Franchise Mode is incredibly addictive. You manage the economy, cleanliness, staff happiness, disease research, animal genetics, and breeding programs so you can eventually release them back into the wild. The game has a wonderful educational aspect and puts a strong emphasis on ecology and conservation, which is highly commendable.

🎧 Audio-Visual Symphony
The graphics are beautiful, the lighting effects at sunset will take your breath away, and the soundtrack is so incredibly pleasant and relaxing that you’ll find yourself listening to it outside of the game. The animal sounds and the ambient noise of the zoo create perfect immersion.

Conclusion
Planet Zoo is a perfect game. It is a combination of deep management, unmatched creative freedom, and pure love for nature. If you love building strategies and animals, this is not a purchase you need to hesitate about. It is a must-have. 10/10
even with planet zoo 2 on the horizon, it still doesnt render this game obsolete. 9.8/10

I'm not recommending this game for one reason. You can't build how you want. The free build is a lie. Paths can only go in set directions. When you place paths and want to connect more paths to it, they wont connect half the time and when they do they wont connect how you want. Also if you have railings on and try to connect a path, the railings block the other path and you just have railings in the middle of your paths. When you try and place a blueprint, you can only place them in 1 meter intervals. This is the only game so far that i have had these problems in. I hope that this problem isn't in there next game.

This game is fantastic. If you loved Zoo Tycoon then you will love this game. I am sad at the fact there is no truly aquatic/marine animals other than seals and sealions but despite that it is very good. I have spent way too many late nights on this game just because seeing how far you can push it is actually very fun. The workshop items by other players make the experience so much better as well. 
I highly recommend this to anyone who just loves to sit down for hours and decorate environments and buildings.

I’ve always loved animals, and I was disappointed when I couldn’t get into the biology and veterinary care class I wanted. Playing this game has honestly been a great alternative because it lets me learn more about the animals I love in a fun, interactive, and educational way. I enjoy discovering different species, learning about their needs and behaviors, and managing everything in a creative sandbox environment.
What makes the game even better is how relaxing and rewarding it feels to build and manage your own space while still learning new things along the way. You can tell that a lot of care went into making the animals feel unique and realistic, which makes the experience even more enjoyable for animal lovers like me.
It’s probably one of my favorite management games I’ve played, and I would definitely recommend it to anyone who loves animals, creativity, and sandbox or management-style games. Whether you want to learn, relax, or just spend time taking care of virtual animals, this game is absolutely worth trying.

I think this game is super fun but they don't teach you how to make the habitats very well. I found myself having to look up how to make habitats for different animals. Other than that I think the game is very well made and a joy to play

This game was really fun while it was working. Now it crashes ALL THE TIME. During startup, during gameplay, ALL THE TIME! I've played for almost 300 hours and purchased all the the DLCs and now it won't run. I've tried every option I can find to fix it and nothing works. My computer is plenty strong enough to handle this game. Even zoos that are still empty are crashing. Frontier has no help to offer, Steam has no help to offer. I'm thinking Planet Zoo 2 will be a waste of money too.
pretty enjoyable building and observing experience. i found it pretty lame that most animals are blocked behind an in game currency that is tedious to acquire.

Convoluted crap that rides on the back of overpriced DLC and a way-too-intricate building system. A sequel's coming soon, odds are it'll be a mess if Frontier's other recent releases are anything to go by. Dig for the old Zoo Tycoon games instead; they're not as visually impressive as Planet Zoo, but the actual gameplay in them is a hell of a lot more fun.

It takes a lot of effort to make the basic task of [i]placing an animal in the sandbox mode[/i] a tedious chore in your zoo game, but here we are.

I have had a good amount of time to form my opinion. I have zero faith in this company to make a functional game. The path system is insanely finicky, you could make an argument that it's actually broken. You are charged an arm and a leg for the base game plus all expansions for a game they do not ever bother to patch or fix. This is not a spiritual successor to Zoo Tycoon 2: Ultimate Edition, it is actually a very cheap and soulless imitation. I am so sad and disappointed they are also about to make this game completely obsolete because they are releasing a 2nd game! Get ready to have everything you do constantly break and bug your game while this company releases DLC after DLC and zero patches or acknowledgement of issues with gameplay. I truly hate this company. Basically just like EA, where they make the games good enough and just don't bother fixing bugs because they are so rich and popular that they don't need to care what people actually want.

It's an addicting game and I love to play it. So why do I not recommend it? MONEY!!!

The base game is $45 even all these years after release. It has a good number of animals, scenes, props, etc but leaves a lot to be desired. Want more? No worries! Choose from any one of EIGHTEEN (18) different DLC, each one costing $10, and each one only providing a few new animals and a few new bits of scenery. In other words if you want the same level of freedom you had two decades ago with Zoo Tycoon, you have to shell out $220! Let that sink in...

I get it, the graphics are great, the interaction is fun, there's a lot more freedom of creativity, etc. etc. etc. But come on! More than $200 for a zoo game??? If half of the proceeds went to nature conservation that'd be one thing, but it doesn't. If you're truly obsessed with animals and zoos, buy the deluxe bundle on the best sale you possibly can. Otherwise don't bother.

1300 hours later and I finally remembered I never wrote a review :D 
This game has something for everyone. Whether its being creative in sandbox and building hyper detailed zoos, running a chaotic franchise, or enjoying the career mode story. I spend most of my time building in sandbox creating semi-realistic or as I call it realistic-ish zoos. I'm always excited for a new DLC to drop so I can have new animals to build for and new building pieces to discover. Honestly has been money well spent. I MORE then got my money's worth. 
Only con it does have a steep learning curve because its so in depth. Whether its learning the controls, building mechanics, or management...it does take some time and sometimes some deep dive research through youtube. But once you get it...you GET IT and can enjoy the game.
I am a decade late to the party, but this is a very good game. Reminds me quite a bit of the halcyon days of Zoo Tycoon 2.
High level of detail with lotsa of interdependencies.  Great learning components for each animal.

i always wanted to buy this because i love sky-view builders games like this but its was too much but when i saw this on sale i bought the deluxe edition and i am loving it so far, even without any DLC its still feels like a proper game, it was exactly what it advertised and well its pretty thorough also extremely fun to build in the game like i made a cool fence for a gardenpatch i made and it was sick👍

Controls can be tricky, but all in all this is a very well build game with insane levels of detail for the animals and scenery. 

Career mode is fun to play and cleverly narrated too.

This game is great for anyone who loves animals or tycoon games! The graphics are great and there are multiple different aspects that allow you to build a variety of different detailed zoos. The campaign modes serve as a great way to both learn the game and challenge yourself, while the sandbox modes allow you to make any zoo you want. You can spend countless hours in this game without ever getting bored.
Its very realistic and just fun to relax and play.

My wife and I have been playing this game for years now, and we can't imagine any other game that we would pay money for every single DLC that comes out. We both played Zoo Tycoon back in the day, this game just amplifies everything we loved about it. The building controls are easy and intuitive, and the management aspects strike a good balance between skill and casual gameplay. Also, Saltwater Croc- best animal.

very good game, but i am really annoyed, i was making a zoo and it was going so well, but then apparently the keepers decides to just stop doing their jobs. their happiness and workloads were fine, as were the work zones, but they kept letting animals starve, and i couldnt do anything about it, i kept requesting a keeper to come urgently but when i did that the keeper would just run around the park doing nothing, or would go in the enclosure do nothing and leave, i even tried hiring loads of keepers but it didnt work, so i tried firing all of them and hiring new ones, didnt work either. so now my zoo is ruined and i cant even do anything about it. even restarting the pc doesnt work :(
BUT other than that its a great game

There's a lot to do, a lot to learn and a lot to experiment with this game, and I'm only talking about the base version of it. If you are new as I was when I played PZ, you may find yourself a bit overwhelmed by everything. Start small, start slow. There's a ton of things to consider, from habitat distribution, coverage and special needs of animals, to guests needs and technical requirements such as energy and water, as well as staff workload efficiency. Animals have their own social, enviromental and dietary needs; as I said before, it's a lot for a beginner, but nonetheless fun once you get the grasp of it! I recommend watching some youtube videos for decorating the habitats. Don't forget to do reasearch with veterinaries and mechanics, as it will allow you to better the conditions of your animals, be better at disease management, and get a ton of new buildings and enrichment objects for the zoo. Also, put ATMs.

TLDR: very cool game, lots of things to consider, don't rush yourself into big habitats. Do reasearch to unlock new and necessary things for the zoo. Have fun!

yes this game is very good and amazing and awesome. ive been wanting it for 4 years now and finally got it :D

definitely way better than I expected

Now that PZ2 has just been announced, I realise I have never reviewed this one. A lot of fun, and trickier than it looks. Who would have known how many things you have to consider to build and run a zoo! 

Maybe it is just my own ineptitude, but my zoos never really look all that professional, and the building and terrain tools can be frustrating, to say the least, but the overall game is a lot of fun
Throughly enjoy game! Love building zoos and expanding on my habitats!

AWESOME AWESOME AWESOME. You do need a couple of guides to understand the basics but once you got the hang of it, it's addicting. Can't wait for the sequel! 100% recommended please play it if you love zoos.
great studio great builds great animals great great great...

Planet Zoo is a great zoo simulator. This game does have good graphics, building mechanics and a soundtrack. Weirdly enough, I think the challenge mode story is very engaging and tough. The online mode, "franchise mode", is also fun, but I wish they did more community challenges even if Planet Zoo 2 releases.
Although the controls are a bit clunky compared to Frontier's more recent outputs, it's still a really fun game. I love the campaign mode and the amount of DLC I can collect :)

Quite a nice, game lots of customisation and such. runs quite well on my pc but does have some noise specs should be attached. can be confusing but you get used to it, game looks really nice and is enjoyable
I was so addicted to this game that I had to stop cold turkey because I was devoting every waking hour to it
I have always been a huge fan of zoo games. This one is one of the best! So much can be done in this amazing game. Super recommend!

Amazing game that is beautifully crafted, I find myself being drawn to this game anytime I am reminded of it. I love the premises of the game, create a zoo, design all of it while balencing beauty and funtionallity, get animals, raise them, breed them, and release them. Meanwhile teaching your guests about animals and earning money from them. My main issue with the game is that the controls take some getting used to and practicing with them. Another issue I have is the amount of DLCs there are, I play a ton of paradox games so it's not a big deal for me. Atleast unlike paradox the DLCs you don't need for gameplay, they are just props and new animals. Besides those two issues the game would be a 10/10.

Can't wait to play Planet zoo 2, I hope they keep the music that was created for the first game and I am looking forward to using wildlife reserves for my animals.

It is pretty good, the graphics could be a bit better from afar, or some of the placement features could be more complete. I think these will be fixed in the new one, so the base game is jsut awesome with all teh animamls and the depth you can go into with them.

Planet Zoo is easily one of the best zoo management games I've ever played.

Just like Planet Coaster, the amount of creativity and freedom you get is incredible. You can build a realistic zoo, create themed areas, design detailed habitats and make every zoo feel completely different from the last.

The animals are the real stars of the game. They look great, behave realistically and require proper care if you want them to thrive. Building habitats that fit their needs is just as important as making them look good for visitors.

There is also a strong management side to the game. You need to keep guests happy, manage your staff, balance your finances and make sure your animals are well cared for. It adds an extra layer to the game and makes your zoo feel like a real, living place.

The DLCs add even more animals, scenery and building pieces, giving you even more options to create the zoo you want.

Whether you enjoy building, management or simply love animals, Planet Zoo offers hundreds of hours of content and creativity.

Even after all this time, it's still a game I keep coming back to.
It is a fun game and it is very relaxing and you can build whatever you whant
This game is so fun and it allows for great creativity
I love having a never ending list of objectives to complete, so far theres no getting bored or complacent.
I love this game, its VERY addicting. I cant wait for the second one to come out. XD
I absolutely love Planet Zoo and it brings back all of the nostalgia of Zoo Tycoon - The building tools are so fun and intuitive you can really let your imagination lose.

I love this game. The building mechanics are a little complicated at times, but the game is so versatile, educational, and fun! I love animals and really wanted to study biology or zoology, but chose a different path in life. I feel like I can live my zoologist dreams through planet zoo! lol
It's super realistic for people who have a great computer but for people like me it's not as realistic but still really fun. I can't stop playing.
Amazing game with such beautiful animals. You never get bored of playing, theres so much to do ranging from customizing your own buildings to creating your own habitats for animals.

The Idea of this game is brillant, but the controls and the camera is not, I have struggled to move around the map, it tells you to do certain steps but you cant move around freely to be able to follow these steps, such a shame as this could be such a good game, I wasted my money on this game.

One of the greatest sim games I have ever played. The beauty of the animals, the design of the environments and architecture... this game has set the standard for sim games. I hope Frontier continues to outdo themselves, and I cannot wait for Planet Zoo 2!!!

i think i spent a decade trying to find an alternative to zoo tycoon 1 and 2. then planet zoo was announced. i've been playing since release and while i agree the dlcs should've been bigger for their price, i also understand that the devs took a lot of time to carefully research those animals so perhaps it's justified after all!
there's smaller bugs here and there, after six years you definitely see it's age, but it's beautiful and fun regardless. i struggle with creativity when it comes to building, so i'm never quite happy with a habitat, but it never felt like wasted time and money! very excited for planet zoo 2, much love to frontier! <3
absolutely love this game, it allows creativity and has amazing creature models. would recommend it to people who love games like JWE and zoo tycoon (obviously), but for sure check it out :)
bought this and found out like an hour later that the second game was announced. Still fun though.
amazing game, graphics are amazing if your device can handle it.
its a grate game but needs more animels and better animal behaver
Great game. Very chill, except for when my zoo is in debt. then im stressed. But I like it! Would recommend as a good cosy game
graphics are so perfect and game is so detailed, cant wait for 2 to come out :D

As a huge animal lover, Planet Zoo was an easy game for me to fall in love with. While it isn’t focused on directly interacting with animals, that’s because it’s first and foremost a management simulator and it does that incredibly well.
One of my favorite things is simply sitting back and watching the animals thrive while seeing visitors enjoy the zoo I’ve built. There’s something really satisfying about creating habitats, improving the park, and seeing everything come together. Sandbox mode is where I spent most of my time, and the freedom it offers is fantastic.
That said, Planet Zoo can be intimidating when you’re starting out. The building tools are incredibly powerful, but they can also feel overly complicated and overwhelming for new players. That’s my biggest hope for Planet Zoo 2 simpler and more intuitive building and management systems without sacrificing creativity.
I already pre ordered Planet Zoo 2 and I’m genuinely excited for it. Frontier has announced improvements along with new features like flying and aquatic animals, which makes the sequel even more exciting.  
My only real complaint is the DLC model. I own Planet Zoo and every DLC, and now I’ll be starting over with a new game and a new wave of DLC purchases to unlock additional animals. I understand why it happens, but it still stings as a long time fan.
Overall, Planet Zoo is one of the best zoo management games ever made. If you love animals, creativity, and building your dream zoo, it’s an easy recommendation.
Good game, realistic animals, informative but pathing system needs a HUGE upgrade
It's like Planet Coaster, but with animals. My favorite Zoo Sim game. Good gameplay, graphics,and plenty to do. You could loose yourself for hours just watching animals and making your Zoo the best.
I don't understand why this game is so addicting to me. It makes hours melt away. It has me entrapped and bewitched.
Super fun to play, reminds me of zoo tycoon that I used to play as a kid but way better graphics and more things and designs and animals I can work with!
I had loved this style of game ever since I was a kid playing Zoo Tycoon 3 on my local library PC. Planet Zoo has enhanced that experience tenfold and I enjoy playing it so much.
So fun for animal lovers, and people who like Sim games. It's engaging and realistic while not being tooo hard.
Its the best game for animal lovers! Even more for the people with zookeeper´s experience.
Amazing opportunities for creativity and so much variation when building zoos. If you like base creation, resource management, and sandbox mode this is def the game for you.
It is nice fun and i will like wake up and get on to the game and look for otters
Amazing and good graphics, my computer can still run it easily though! Very fun and addictive. hope this review help people find the most fun game on the planet.

Game is very fun and full of content, just overwhelming at times! <3

I just got the game today but so far I can say it's pretty fun, there is lots of content and I have about 5 hours on the game so far. The information on the animals is great and if anyone wants to play a fun game that has some educational stuff about animals, this is a pretty good game for that. I love the models for the animals as well! Humans...not so much...   
The building is pretty fun! It is a little confusing at first and may be hard to navigate but as you make your way through the tutorials it gets easier. Everything else is pretty easy to navigate.
I got a few DLCs and I am excited to try those out and see more content, I think there is plenty of content without any DLCs though. 
Only thing I have to say is: if you get overwhelmed easily, this game can get pretty overwhelming. I have always come back though, it is not so overwhelming you wouldn't wanna just take a break and come back. So, I suggest getting this game if you like animals or want to learn more about animals, or enjoy tycoon like games in a more realistic setting.
Thanks to the developers for both making a game I love and ruining my life by getting my toddler obsessed with their game.

Great game if you enjoy building and simulation. Despite being fun it can get laggy after a while, especially if you have a huge zoo. The game can be hard at times, but as long as you play well and learn it should not be an issue. So overall great and fun game, beefy computer and good money management is recommended

I love this game so much! Time goes so fast when I play it, and I just love it so much overall! Not only do you get to be creative, but you get to learn about taking control over a zoo and you get to design homes for many differents animals from all over the world!
Love love love this game!! The animals are super cute and i enjoy building my own zoo. Would totally reccomend!
It is such a fun game to lose yourself in for hours. When packs are on sale it's a great treat to enhance your game experience even more. Would highly recommend

When I was a kid I played zoo tycoon religiously for as long as my mom would allow me computer time.

This game is like a modern, ethical zoo tycoon. Very very fun!

I nearly missed the viewing to buying my house because I was playing Planet Zoo and lost track of time. That is my review. It is a great game, sometimes I forget to blink because I've been building too long.

This game is amazing for anyone who enjoys animals and creative games! There's so much stuff you can build and design, and it's not constrictive like a lot of sandbox games are. They give you many pieces to build with, and the game mechanics are great. One thing I will say is that some of the animal designs are a bit rough or get boring after a while of seeing the same thing over and over. I wish the coat patterns and markings on certain animals were more diverse, or there was more variation in body build and things like that. Aside from that, this game is great, and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a fun, creative outlet that incorporates animals and conservation.

This game is so good ! If you enjoy the freedom of building your own zoo and see the animal walking around , you should definitely consider getting this game ! I am really looking forward the release of Planet Zoo 2 .

I wrote out so much and accidently deleted it, so Ill rewrite it in a shorter way.
This game is incredible, but new players should definitely wait for discounts before blowing money away. The DLC's make the game so much more entertaining, there is so much you can create. The animals are incredibly realistic, but i do think the older ones/ most of the ones that come with the game no DLC's could use some work. The pathing and building can be annoying sometimes, but other then that it is pretty easy. I love the natural habitats you can make, the nature options are very broad There's sandbox mode and normal mode so you don't have to worry about costs and just free roam the game. I think the terrain can be annoying, depending on where you are is how the terrain will look. Its a fun and relaxing game, i definitely recommend it.

It's fun to play and it has amazing graphics and overall is an amazing game. It's amazing how Frontier gives information about the species that goes in their school and that they help educate the players during the playtime.

I really enjoy this game overall, there are some bugs that have been known for a while that kind of ruin the game play (water depth & climbing) but, other than that i think there's a lot of variety into the game with cultures and themes etc.. and of course the animals. I don't have anything negative to say entirely about the game other than the DLC being $10 USD a piece (and of course the known bugs).... def not worth it and especially since there's SO much DLC to buy i think it's pretty pricy if you want multiple at once!

The best zoo simulator available in my opinion, the animals, the environments, the animations, everything is just beautiful. Highly recommend for any animal lovers, zoo lovers or even just management sim lovers!

I came to this game after playing their older title, Zoo Tycoon, hoping for more freedom, more animals, and deeper customization and honestly, this feels like the perfect evolution of that idea. The game gives you an incredible amount of creative freedom and attention to detail, allowing you to build your dream zoo with almost no restrictions, which is exactly why it works so well.
If you’re thinking about buying it, I’d definitely say it’s worth the price. However, keep in mind that building and designing your zoo is a huge part of the experience. If you prefer a more casual zoo game with minimal management or you’re not really into spending time building, this might not be the game for you.
I also own all of the DLC packs. They’re absolutely not necessary to enjoy the game, but they add so much extra content; unique animals, new challenges, additional building pieces, and more creative possibilities. I’d highly recommend them as well, especially during sales when you can grab several at once for a great price.
They really did an amazing job with this game. Definitely recommend it.

This game is super fun, would recommend if you love animals and enjoy task management/games with a currency system. Anyways I have a pretty bad quality laptop, however it still managed to be super high quality. The attention to detail and graphics of this game are straight up insane. Not only is the animation of the animals super good, but also the models in most of them! Would totally recommend getting the game, even just the base game without any DLC have a wide range of different animals.
Back to the review though, can say with complete confidence that this game is great!
things can def be improved, as with most/all games...but overall i love this game and i recommend downloading it!
What can I say, this game is 10/10, everything you could possibly want. Zoo Tycoon for the modern age!

Um... it is really good to have a west African lion pride because they breed. Also put like one male and three females for starters and don't forget to change the male every time the first litter is matured.

I used to play Zoo Tycoon on the Xbox 360 as a kid and i always really enjoyed it so this game was an amazing experience and having also played planet zoo i already knew that the game would be very nice to play

I really enjoy this game now, it allows you to be creative and actively manage a zoo (and trust me- you need to actively manage it). If I had one piece of feedback- sometimes it's hard to build and I wish when I put injured outsiders into quarentine they'd stay there so they didnt automatically get hurt again
Very good game. I like the selection and would recommend it as you can buy it sometimes for very cheap all dlcs for around 1.4 times the original base game price. definitley recommend.

It's actually quite a decent game. I just can't stand how bad I am in building in this game. DLCs are also quite scammish and money grabbing. But I have fun in constantly breeding endangred animals to save them from extinction. 

7/10
The game is much fun if you want to chill this game is for you make your own designs and your own zoo
Lovely game, open to your deepest expanse of creativity. Best zoo game you'll ever encounter that allows you FULL control of your animals and decoration and habitats :)

Love this game! 
Yes it can be a bit clunky sometimes, but often simulation games like this are really boring, whereas this one has some pretty challenging scenarios.
This game has changes by life for the better and will always be one of my Favorites

Less than half of the people who have bought and installed Planet Zoo have completed the first tutorial level. I’m not surprised. This isn’t a bad game, but it is so fiddly in a number of ways. 

I do understand this game would be more fiddly than Planet Coaster; after all, it is much harder to keep animals alive than it is to keep a roller coaster functioning. However, I wasn’t expecting so much crap do deal with. Most animals need hard shelter, which seems impossible to keep up with. You can have a herd of a dozen zebras, but you will need so much hard shelter than you will wonder how the species exists in nature. I have a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hotel complex here and they are still complaining, so what the hell do zebras to in real life when it rains? Also, how does a tree called Umbrella Tree not create shelter? We should be told which trees create shelter and a LOT more of them should.

When filtering habitat items, they show EVERY species in the game and there’s no option to just show the species your zoo has. I don’t want to have to scroll through dozens and dozens of species just to get to Timber Wolves when that’s the only species I have in my zoo. 

The one-way glass has a 50/50 chance of going the wrong way and you have to manually flip it. Why would I EVER want to stop my customers from seeing the animals and not the other way around? The game should use the door to determine which side is which. 

I should be able to mass select items and then SELL THEM, not just delete them. I need the money, so if it would cost me money to remove it (mostly trees) then leave it there and I will move it for free. 

You can keep different species in the same habitat, and the game will tell you when these species will receive an interspecies enrichment bonus. However, that’s the only information you get. There’s no way to tell in game if two species are fully compatible, i.e., they won’t fight and their habitat needs overlap enough to be at 100%. Or even if they are semi-compatible, i.e., they won’t fight, but some of their habitat needs don’t mesh and you will need to compromise. This should absolutely be in the game. 

Another thing, while playing the campaign, you will often get gift animals and they are almost exclusively garbage. Furthermore, they are territorial and you can’t sell or release gift animals, so you are stuck with them till they die. “Thank you for this gift. I will put them in a habitat, but as soon as there’s another alpha male, I will stick them in storage where they will stay till they die.” This seems at odds with their pro-animal welfare message. 

And the worst part about this... The game is still really good. If they improved the UI a little and gave the users more information about compatibility and shelter than this would be an amazing game. As it is, I can see myself going back and playing again to get another 10 or so achievements, but I can never see myself putting in the effort to 100% the game.
Fun to build!! A lot harder than I thought to start, but I am getting the hang of it.

Planet Zoo is one of the best management sims I’ve played. The level of detail in the animals, habitats, and zoo building is incredible, and it’s both relaxing and rewarding to grow your park over time. The customization options are deep without feeling overwhelming, and watching guests react to your zoo makes it feel alive. Perfect game if you enjoy creativity, strategy, and animals.
Love this game!!!! So good. They just need to review how the North Island Brown Kiwi's habitat looks as they are nocturnal animals. Otherwise. I love playing this game easy to learn how to play too.

Pathing sucks and it is impossible to just build because there are all these riles you need to know to put down exhibits and paths that make the game not fun.  I tried to create a walk through exhibit and no matter what I did the paths would not connect in the exhibit.  it not fun

Very good game, taught me a lot about wildlife and even native plant life. While yes, there are paid DLC, they aren't required to have fun like in most games, and the game is still playable and fun without them. It caters to people willing to spend hours placing small details around every corner and crevice, and to those just wanting to conjure up a quick and easy enclosure with the barriers available in-game. The controls and building system make take a bit to get used to, so I recommend taking time in sandbox just figuring out all the mechanics or playing the tutorial before trying on your own. 11/10 game, 100% would recommend.
Game is fine. Enjoyable time killer. Really wish they didn't DLC so many creatures. Wouldn't recommend unless you can get like a good chunk of this stuff on deep discount.
It was fun and I loved the protests which made it so much more relistect

It blows.... my mind, with how crappy... my life was before I learned of this game. I hate it.... That I lived so many years without it. Screw you.... For making such a great game. I hope you die... A peaceful death surrounded by your loved ones.

If the base price of a game is gonna be $45 USD, there should be free DLC's. Not almost $10 per DLC. 

Games OKAY but I don't think its worth all that money you spend. The Hunter Call Of The Wild is $20 AND has free DLC's, and I get to see all the animals to, $45 for a zoo creator + $10 per DLC isn't worth it.
